message:
test(fcp-core): pin RequestId Display roundtrip variants (abr7b)

Add crates/fcp-core/tests/request_id_display_roundtrip.rs.
RequestId(String) is the Hub↔connector wire correlation id; the
docs (protocol.rs:30-32) explicitly say the format is unprescribed.
13/13 tests pin Display + FromStr (Infallible) + serde JSON
roundtrip across the documented variants.

Variants covered: random monotonic (req_<20-zero-padded>), short
user-supplied (req_123), UUID hyphenated, UUID simple, opaque
correlation tokens, mixed-case, single char, and empty string.

Pins:
- Display emits constructor input verbatim across all variants.
- Display idempotent under repeat formatting.
- Display→FromStr round-trip preserves Eq + Hash (FromStr is
  Infallible).
- Equality + hash agree across new(&str) / new(String) / FromStr /
  From<&str> / From<String> / clone / Display roundtrip
  construction paths.
- RequestId::random() emits "req_<20-digit-sequence>" with monotonic
  distinct values that round-trip cleanly.
- Distinct inputs distinct RequestIds.
- HashMap-key correctness across construction paths (incl. empty).
- Serde JSON form is the bare quoted string (newtype optimization)
  with format pinned exactly via "req_pinned" anchor.
- pub-field access (id.0) matches Display.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
